author: śrīla saccidānanda bhaktibinoda ṭhākura
verses:
prabhu! tava padayuge mora nivedana
nāhi māgi deha⋅sukha, vidyā, dhana, jana

nāhi māgi svarga, āra mokṣa nāhi māgi
nā kari prārthanā kona vibhūtira lāgi

nija⋅karma⋅guṇa⋅doṣe je je⋅janma pāi
janme janme jena tava nāma⋅guṇa gāi

eimātra āśā mama, tomāra caraṇe
ahaitukī bhakti hṛde jāge anukṣaṇe

viṣaye je prīti ebe āchaye āmāra
sei mata prīti houka, caraṇe tomāra

vipade sampade tāhā thākuka samabhāve
dine dine vṛddhi houka nāmera prabhāve

paśupakṣī haye thāki svarge vā niraye
tava bhakti rahu bhaktivinoda⋅hṛdaye
en_translation:
O Lord! This is my humble submission at Your lotus feet: I do not ask for bodily happiness, knowledge, wealth, or followers.

I do not ask for heaven, nor do I ask for liberation. I do not pray for any opulence.

By the merits and demerits of my own karma, whatever births I may receive, may I, birth after birth, sing Your names and glories.

This is my only hope at Your feet: that causeless devotion awakens in my heart at every moment.

Whatever affection I currently have for sense objects, may that same affection be for Your feet.

May that affection remain equal in distress and prosperity, and may it increase day by day by the influence of Your Holy Name.

Whether I become an animal or a bird, whether I am in heaven or in hell, may devotion to You remain in Bhaktivinoda's heart.
